svg.text: Separate top-to-bottom pipeline stages. FTQ is drawn as storage, not as a pipeline stage.
Critical path: redirect priority, uBTB/RAS target, fallthrough.
BP0 -> BP1 pipeline register: fetch_pc, fallback_pc, redirect/valid state
BP1 / F1: lane PCs, uBTB, RAS, fast target
Fast direction/target estimate for common taken control flow.
BP1 -> BP2 pipeline register: lanes, uBTB hit/meta, RAS top, fast target
BP2 / F2: main predictor correction and FTQ enqueue
Large/slower predictors validate or override the fast uBTB result.
RAS for ret, ITTAGE for indirect
FTQ is storage, not a pipeline stage
It decouples prediction from fetch and keeps metadata alive until update/recovery.
one entry per predicted fetch block
pred target / CFI lane / CFI type
head may advance after fetch consume
FTQ does not store instruction bytes
Bytes live in I-cache / fetch return / instruction buffer.
FTQ stores prediction metadata, lifecycle metadata, and rollback snapshots.
IF0: fetch PC selection and I-cache request
Consumes FTQ head; redirect has priority over queued prediction.
IF0 -> IF1 pipeline register: if_pc, ftq_id, start offset, cache index
IF1: I-TLB and I-cache tag/data arrays
VIPT-style overlap: VA index/data access while translation returns PA tag.
IF1 -> IF2 pipeline register: line bytes, hit/fault, ftq_id, fetch_pc
IF2: align, RVC predecode, CFI predecode, instruction buffer
Converts bytes into instruction entries for decode while preserving FTQ metadata.
IF2 -> Decode pipeline register / instruction buffer read side
1. BP0 PC MUX -> uBTB/RAS -> fast target -> next fetch PC
2. BP2 main BTB/TAGE tag match -> redirect correction
3. IF1 I-TLB + I-cache tag/data -> IF2 aligner
4. IF2 RVC prefix network + CFI predecode
5. FTQ snapshot restore -> predictor history/RAS repair
Halfword lanes are length-classified in parallel.
start_mask is generated by combinational prefix/mask logic.
A 32b instruction consumes two halfwords; the second halfword is not a start.
Cross-line/page instructions need saved first half and precise fault metadata.
Instruction-buffer entries carry PC, canonical inst, is_rvc, exception, ftq_id.
2. Main BTB/TAGE correction overrides fast uBTB if needed.
3. Return uses RAS top when CFI type says return.
4. Indirect jump target can come from ITTAGE.
5. Otherwise direct BTB target or fetch fallthrough is used.
Use this diagram for implementation/timing discussion. It is a modern reference sketch, not a claim that every core implements every optional block exactly this way.
